Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christina Gallagher Nelson for Michael Kasolas, Petitioner and Successor Trustee.
James Picetti, beneficiary and surviving spouse of Benjamin Picetti, in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Remove Trust from Court Supervision.

The Court stated it had reviewed the petition and inquired whether there was any objection to the relief sought by the petition. Ms. Nelson stated that she had nothing to add to the petition. Mr. Picetti stated that he was in support of the petition so long as it expedited distribution to beneficiaries.

After discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, First Accounting of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Continued To Nov-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christina Gallagher for successor trustee and petitioner Michael Kasolas, who was also present (via Zoom).
Caroline Hinshaw for conservatee (deceased) Benjamin Picetti (via Zoom).
James Picetti, surviving spouse of Benjamin Picetti (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Approval of Successor Trustee's First Account and Report, Payment of Fees for First Account Period, and Payment of Attorney's Fees and Costs for First Account Period.

The Court inquired whether Mr. Kasolas was married to Stella Pantazis, a court staff attorney and general counsel for the San Francisco Superior Court. Mr. Kasolas confirmed that he was. The Court stated that after reviewing the applicable judicial ethics standards, it was determined that it would be inappropriate to proceed and therefore recused itself from the case. The Court further noted that the recusal would extend to the entire bench of the San Francisco Superior Court.

The Court indicated that the matter would need to be continued to allow the matter to be heard by a visiting judge. After discussing scheduling, the matter was continued to November 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Paul Melbostad appeared by zoom in propria persona (accounting petition).
Brett Schweinburg appeared by zoom for and with Paul Melbostad by zoom.
Edward Kaplowitz appeared by zoom for James Picetti.

Mr. Wisch explained the difference in their representation of the successor trustee. The Court noted Caroline Hinshaw was not present. Mr. Melbostad explained services provided and time spent to support his fee request, provided history and spoke of circumstances of elder abuse that occurred with Mr. Picetti, spoke of the amount of money that was taken from Mr. Picetti, spoke of amount of the amount of losses he prevented, the amount of risk and liability he took on during the time he was trustee and explained the actions he took to protect Mr. Picetti and to recover and protect his assets, etc. Mr. Prueitt and Mr. Wisch presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to Mr. Melbostad's fees being unreasonable, excessive, unauthorized, and some of the services provided being those of a trustee rather than an attorney.
